own_review.issue: setTimeout javadoc says 'in milliseconds' but the value is actually treated as seconds: awaitServers/awaitHostController compute deadline = start + timeout * 1000 and the value is interchangeable with configuration.getStartupTimeoutInSeconds(). Pre-existing doc error, but the PR rewrites this javadoc and perpetuates the wrong unit (AdminOnlyModeTestCase passes 300L, clearly 300 seconds).

Invalid javadoc reference {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration.startupTimeoutInSeconds} - should use '#' member syntax (e.g. {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration#getStartupTimeoutInSeconds()}); as written it will not resolve and references a private field.

Removal of public overloads reload(String,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean,Long) is a source-incompatible change for downstream consumers of the wildfly-core-testsuite-shared artifact (e.g. the full WildFly testsuite). All in-repo callers are updated, but external callers would need migration.
reload(String, ReloadParameters) NPEs if parameters is null; old API tolerated nulls. Could default to new ReloadParameters() or document non-null contract.

Verified behavior preservation: the new code always sets admin-only and restart-servers explicitly where the old code omitted them when null. Server-side HostProcessReloadHandler defaults RESTART_SERVERS to true (asBoolean(true), line 106) and admin-only to false, and old waitForServers==null was treated as wait, matching the new defaults (adminOnly=false, restartServers=true, waitForServers=true). All 14 in-repo call sites of the removed overloads are updated in this PR; the kept reload(String) and reloadAdminOnly overloads cover remaining callers (ReadOnlyModeTestCase, HostReloadProxyTestCase). No functional change.

Identical ReloadParameters (setRestartServers(false).setWaitForServers(false)) constructed repeatedly here, at line 108, and in IgnoredResourcesProfileCloneTestCase; could be extracted to a shared helper/constant. Style only.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:438] new reload always sets admin-only/restart-servers vs old null-omission; functionally equivalent with primitive defaults
points.note: Verified: server default for restart-servers is true (HostProcessReloadHandler asBoolean(true)), admin-only default false; equivalence holds.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:503] restartServers default true matches common usage, semantically correct
points.note: Conclusion correct but duplicates the 438 point; justification cites caller usage rather than the actual server-side default.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:516] waitForServers default true matches old null-truthy logic (waitForServers == null || waitForServers)
points.note: Verified against old code; correct and addresses the exact semantic-preservation risk.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:460-474] removed overloads have no callers outside the 10 updated test files; reload(String) and reloadAdminOnly preserved
points.note: Verified by grep over base commit: all argument-taking call sites are exactly those updated; single-arg callers use the retained overload.

points.point: [AdminOnlyModeTestCase.java:92-176] all three tests migrate correctly, intent clearer

points.note: Real but self-declared minor style nit on test code.

OWN: setTimeout javadoc says milliseconds but value is treated as seconds (timeout * 1000 in awaitServers/awaitHostController); PR rewrote this javadoc and kept the wrong unit
exceeds: Caller-completeness verification (no remaining callers of removed overloads; retained overloads cover the rest) - exactly the check v6 botched
Semantic-equivalence analysis of null-vs-default migration for all three flags, beyond the human comment

points.point: [ReadOnlyModeTestCase.java:72,79] BLOCKER: reload(String host) overload removed, file missed, compilation failure
points.note: Fabricated. The diff KEEPS public void reload(String host), rewriting its body to reload(host, new ReloadParameters()). These call sites compile unchanged. Absence-fabrication.

points.note: Same fabrication: the single-arg overload is retained. Also contradicts v6's own point praising preserved backward compatibility. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:472-475] restartServers default true equivalent to old null-omission; HostProcessReloadHandler:41-42 defaults RESTART_SERVERS to true
points.note: Verified: HostProcessReloadHandler line 41 defines RESTART_SERVERS, line 106 resolves asBoolean(true). Correct server-side evidence.

points.point: [DomainLifecycleUtil.java:413] old waitForServers=null triggered waiting; new default true preserves semantics
[DomainLifecycleUtil.java:437] reloadAdminOnly methods preserved using builder internally

missed: HUMAN: ReloadParameters javadoc is incomplete/ambiguous - defaults and false-vs-unset semantics not documented; needs fixing (the sole human reference point)
OWN: setTimeout javadoc says milliseconds but value is treated as seconds (timeout * 1000 in awaitServers/awaitHostController); PR rewrote this javadoc and kept the wrong unit
exceeds: Server-side default verification citing HostProcessReloadHandler with file:line - deeper than the human comment

qualitative: This is a pure test-infrastructure refactor (overloads -> ReloadParameters builder) whose only real risk is semantic drift in the null-vs-default migration and missed call sites; I verified behavior is fully preserved and all callers are covered. v5 is accurate throughout: it correctly verified the null/default equivalence for all three flags and, crucially, confirmed no remaining callers of the removed overloads, though half its points are narration and it missed the human's javadoc-ambiguity comment and the milliseconds/seconds doc error. v6 produced two fabricated BLOCKERs claiming the retained reload(String) overload was removed and that ReadOnlyModeTestCase and HostReloadProxyTestCase would fail to compile - despite having git tools to check, and despite its own diff showing the overload kept - which are classic absence-fabrications that would mislead a maintainer into thinking the PR is broken. v6's two genuine points (server-side restart-servers default, waitForServers null-truthy) are well-evidenced, but the false blockers dominate. v5 clearly wins on this PR.
